strengths: Identifies three distinct real-world objects and attempts to link each to the prescribed IA prompt.
Provides basic real-world contexts for each object, demonstrating that the objects are not purely generic.
Includes a correctly formatted source for one object, showing an effort to ground arguments in evidence.
weaknesses: Contexts remain vague and implied rather than clearly and specifically articulated.
Links between objects and the IA prompt are largely descriptive, repetitive and lack depth in TOK concepts (AOKs, WOKs, perspectives).
Assertions about how values shape knowledge are unsupported by detailed evidence or clear argumentation, limiting the analytical rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The examinerâs band-level feedback places this work at the lower end of the 3â4 band: while three objects and basic contexts are identified and linked to the prompt, the links are unconvincing, explanations are superficial and repetitive, and there is minimal analytical depth. These characteristics align precisely with the descriptor for a score of 3. Given the official, non-negotiable total of 3 marks and the relative quality shown in the strand assessment, the criterion score is set at 3.

annotations.comment: The link between the gym and the prompt is asserted but repetitive and unsupported. Provide concrete examplesâsuch as a specific nutrition programâand analyze how the underlying values influenced the knowledge produced within that context.

The term âvaluesâ is used without definition in a TOK context. Offer a concise working definition of âvaluesâ within knowledge acquisition to ground your argument and clarify how specific values guide the pursuit of knowledge.

The photograph of the laptop is visually clear but generic. To better illustrate its educational role, consider including an image of the screen in useâsuch as displaying a course platformâto directly link the object to your argument.
The final sentence is incomplete. Ensure that your description fully explains how and why the laptop was acquired for educational purposes, and proofread to prevent truncated statements.
The heading for Object 2 should be clearly separated from the prior discussion. Use distinct formatting or spacing to delineate each objectâs section for improved structural clarity.

The link to autonomy is stated but does not engage with deeper TOK considerations (such as ethical implications or the role of responsibility). Expand your discussion by connecting autonomy to broader TOK issues.

Claims about portability improving learning lack supporting evidence or references. Incorporate at least one credible sourceâsuch as educational technology researchâto substantiate how a laptopâs mobility affects knowledge acquisition.

The real-world context for the geocentric model is overly general. Specify a particular time, place or institution (e.g., the medieval Church in 13th-century Europe) to ground your object in its historical setting.

The discussion of resistance to heliocentrism lacks primary evidence. Cite a historical example (e.g., Galileoâs trial) and analyze how religious values concretely impeded acceptance of new astronomical knowledge.

The context description âa gym near my homeâ remains vague. Specify details such as the gymâs name, its community demographics or particular programs attended to anchor the object in a precise real-world scenario and support deeper analysis.

The object title is clear but lacks an explicit reference to the TOK prompt. Consider adding a brief rationale here to signal from the outset how the gym connects to the question about values affecting knowledge acquisition.

Although the prompt is stated, it is not restated or integrated into the introduction of Object 1. Reintroduce the question in your commentary to reinforce alignment between each object and the prescribed IA prompt.

The definition of geocentric theory is too simplistic. In a TOK context, situate this belief within Ways of Knowing like faith or authority to explore how these values influenced early scientific knowledge.

The image of the gym appears highly generic and unbranded. To strengthen the real-world context, include a photograph that clearly identifies the specific facility (e.g., signage or unique interior features) so that the object is unmistakably grounded in its actual setting.

This discussion remains highly descriptive and does not draw on TOK frameworks (e.g., Areas of Knowledge or Ways of Knowing). Integrate specific TOK terminology to move from description to analysis of how values shape knowledge.
The reference to âNikolay Kopernikâ is incorrect; the accepted Latinized name is Nicolaus Copernicus. Use accurate names for historical figures to uphold academic precision and credibility.

The conclusion restates earlier points but does not synthesize the distinct contributions of each object. Strengthen your closing by comparing how each object uniquely illuminates the relationship between values and knowledge.

Only one source is listed despite multiple objects requiring support. Include references for the gymâs programs, laptop learning studies and historical texts on geocentric theory to substantiate your claims.

This concluding paragraph for Object 1 largely repeats previous points without adding new insight. Condense or eliminate redundancy and focus on deepening your analysis or transitioning clearly to Object 2.

This description of portability remains general and does not reference specific TOK Ways of Knowing (e.g., âlanguage,â âmemoryâ). Integrate these concepts to anchor your analysis in TOK terminology.

The phrase âknowledge carrier perspectiveâ is introduced without explanation or reference to TOK theory. Define this concept or substitute established TOK terminology to clarify how it relates to your analysis.

The heading for Object 3 should use consistent academic formatting (e.g., italicize âGeocentric theoryâ) and clearly signal the shift in focus from the previous object for better structural coherence.

This in-text source attribution lacks a corresponding citation format linked to the bibliography. Use consistent parenthetical citations (e.g., (Editors of Britannica, 2023)) to connect your references clearly.

The geocentric diagram is visually rich but the analysis does not engage with its key features (e.g., epicycles, concentric spheres). Highlight specific elements and explain how they exemplify the role of values in shaping astronomical knowledge.
The conclusion lacks critical reflection on alternative perspectives or limitations. Consider addressing potential counterarguments or acknowledging the scope of your analysis to demonstrate depth.

The bibliography section is minimal. Cite additional sources to support your analysis of each objectâsuch as scholarly studies on community learning, educational technology or historical scienceâto demonstrate thorough research.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
